title: Calibration Protocol for OD600 Inter-equipment Conversion with LUDOX
author: Paul Rutten
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5gig3ue

steps.title: Add 100 μl LUDOX CL-X into...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μl LUDOX CL-X into wells A1, B1, C1, D1 1ml LUDOX CL-X Contributed by users

steps.title: Add 100 μl of ddH20 into...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μl of ddH20 into wells A2, B2, C2, D2 ddH20 Contributed by users

steps.title: Measure absorbance at 600 nm of...
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at 600 nm of all samples in the measurement mode you plan to use for cell measurements Plate reader (Generic, .)

steps.title: Record the data in your notebook...
steps.instruction: Record the data in your notebook or in the table below: %s %s

steps.title: Once you've collected your results, open...
steps.instruction: Once you've collected your results, open this spreadsheet to process them. iGEM 2019 Plate Reader LUDOX Calibration.xlsx

steps.title: Yellow cells will automatically populate with...
steps.instruction: Yellow cells will automatically populate with values calculated from your raw data. Here is an example of a completed table: Example of a completed data analysis table for this experiment. Blue cells contain raw data, yellow cells are the result of automatic calculations based on that data. The corrected Abs600 is calculated by subtracting the H2O reading. The reference OD600 is defined as that measured by the reference spectrophotometer (as provided to you in the Excel sheet). The correction factor to convert measured Abs600 to OD600 is thus the Reference OD600 divided by Abs600. All cell density readings using this instrument with the same settings and volume can be converted to OD600 by multiplying by (in this example) 1.585.

steps.title: Input your raw values from Step...
steps.instruction: Input your raw values from Step 5 into the blue cells in the table. go to step #4

steps.title: You have now completed this calibration protocol
steps.instruction: You have now completed this calibration protocol
